segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Which actor read the letter in the end, the aristocrat or the jailer?

translations.zh-Hans: 最后是谁读了信，是贵族还是狱卒？

segments.text: Some plays are so successful that they run for years on end.

translations.zh-Hans: 有些戏剧非常成功，会连续上演很多年。

segments.text: In many ways, this is unfortunate for the poor actors who are required to go on repeating the same lines night after One would expect them to know their parts by heart and never have cause to falter.

translations.zh-Hans: 在许多方面，这对那些可怜的演员很不幸，因为他们不得不一夜又一夜地重复同样的台词。人们本以为他们会把角色背得滚瓜烂熟，绝不会支吾。

segments.text: Yet this is not always the case.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，事实并非总是如此。

segments.text: A famous actor in a highly successful play was once cast in the role of an aristocrat who had been imprisoned in the Bastille for twenty years.

translations.zh-Hans: 一位著名演员曾在一部非常成功的戏中扮演一名被囚禁在巴士底狱20年的贵族。

segments.text: In the last act, a jailer would always come onto the stage with a letter which he would hand to the prisoner.

translations.zh-Hans: 最后一幕中，狱卒总会拿着一封信上台，把信交给囚犯。

segments.text: Even though the noble was expected to read the letter at each performance, he always insisted that it should be written out in full.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管这位贵族每场演出都要读信，他却总坚持要把信的全文写出来。

segments.text: One night, the jailer decided to play a joke on his colleague to find out if, after so many performances, he had managed to learn the contents of the letter by heart.

translations.zh-Hans: 一天晚上，狱卒决定捉弄一下同事，看看演了这么多场以后，他是否已经把信的内容背熟。

segments.text: The curtain went up on the final act of the play and revealed the aristocrat sitting alone behind bars in his dark cell.

translations.zh-Hans: 最后一幕拉开帷幕，只见那位贵族独自坐在阴暗牢房的铁栏后面。

segments.text: Just then, the jailer appeared with the precious letter in his hands.

translations.zh-Hans: 这时，狱卒手持那封珍贵的信出现了。

segments.text: He entered the cell and presented the letter to the aristocrat.

translations.zh-Hans: 他走进牢房，把信交给贵族。

segments.text: But the copy he gave him had not been written out in full as usual.

translations.zh-Hans: 可这次交给他的信不像往常那样写有全文。

segments.text: It was simply a blank sheet of paper.

translations.zh-Hans: 那只是一张白纸。

segments.text: The jailer looked on eagerly, anxious to see if his fellow actor had at last learned his lines.

translations.zh-Hans: 狱卒急切地看着，想知道同台演员是否终于背熟了台词。

segments.text: The noble stared at the blank sheet of paper for a few seconds.

translations.zh-Hans: 贵族盯着白纸看了几秒钟。

segments.text: Then, squinting his eyes, he said, The light is dim.

translations.zh-Hans: 接着他眯起眼睛说：“光线太暗了。”

segments.text: Read the letter to me.

translations.zh-Hans: “把信念给我听。”

segments.text: And he promptly handed the sheet of paper to the jailer.

translations.zh-Hans: 说完便立即把纸递给狱卒。

segments.text: Finding that he could not remember a word of the letter either, the jailer replied, The light is indeed dim, Sire.

translations.zh-Hans: 狱卒发现自己也记不起信中的一个字，只好回答：“陛下，光线确实很暗。”

segments.text: I must get my glasses.

translations.zh-Hans: “我得去取眼镜。”

segments.text: With this, he hurried off the stage.

translations.zh-Hans: 说罢，他匆匆离开舞台。

segments.text: Much to the aristocrat's amusement, the jailer returned a few moments later with a pair of glasses and the usual copy of the letter, which he proceeded to read to the prisoner.

translations.zh-Hans: 令贵族忍俊不禁的是，几分钟后狱卒戴着一副眼镜、拿着平常那份信回来了，随后把信念给囚犯听。
